title: What Seductive Deceiver Calls Mercy
dramatic_function: turns intimacy into the delivery system for danger

signifier_package.room_logic: domestic legitimacy is assembled by hand
signifier_package.object_logic: the image is where the lie congeals

dialogue_grammar.speech_pattern: every intimate line can also be a threat

narrative_payoff.resolution: intimacy must be recognized as infrastructure and exited deliberately

opening_image: The threat becomes obvious only after the room, policy, or system has already taken their side.
escalation_pattern: uses charisma and spectacle to cover mediocrity or fraud
sample_lines: Trust me. I built this room for us.
Only you would understand why this had to happen.
hooks: The villain does not want to win a fight. They want to redefine what counts as winning.
The villain does not want to win a fight. They want to redefine what counts as winning.
The villain's true weapon is not force but the ability to make harm sound responsible.
